title: Fix runtime users.json path to use mounted /data volume
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the deploy script mounts /volume1/docker/topcastles/data into container path /data, but the Node user API currently writes users.json relative to process.cwd(), which resolves to /app/server/data/users.json in the container. That leaves the NAS-mounted /data directory unused and causes favorites to be lost on container rebuild. Fix the runtime path resolution so production writes to the mounted /data/users.json path while preserving a safe local-development fallback.

title: Deploy: preflight NAS image directory before container replacement
Fix container Node entrypoint path
description: Container crashes with MODULE_NOT_FOUND because Node tries to load /app/server/server/index.js. Fix the minimal Docker/compose/package startup path so the existing single-container Node server starts at the verified server/index.js entrypoint.
acceptance_criteria: Container starts without MODULE_NOT_FOUND; Node server remains the single container entrypoint; static page, API health, SPA fallback, and deep links work.

title: Phase 14.5: Favorites UX improvements + navigation
description: Optimistic update in addCastleToSet, snackbar feedback on add, bookmark icons in castle list, show-favorites-only filter, nav links for Favorites+Account, account placeholder page.
close_reason: Optimistic addCastleToSet, snackbar on add, isFavorite() in grid+table, show-favorites-only toggle, Favorites+Account nav in toolbar+sidenav, account placeholder page, test fixes.

title: Phase 14.4: Add castle to favorites (detail page, selectable set)
description: Add 'Add to favorites' UI to castle detail page. Inline set picker (no modal). addCastleToSet() in FavoritesService handles dedup. Edge cases: no sets yet, castle already in set.
close_reason: bookmark_add icon + mat-menu set picker in title-row. addCastleToSet() added to FavoritesService. Disabled if already in set, empty-state message if no sets.

title: Phase 14.3: Angular integration (user + favorites sets UI)
description: Create UserService (token management, register/me API calls) and FavoritesService (signal-based state, CRUD). Add /favorites page with Angular Material UI. Auto-register user on first visit. No changes to existing pages.
close_reason: UserService and FavoritesService created. FavoritesPage at /favorites with create/delete. Auto-register on first visit. Build clean, no new test failures.

title: Phase 14.2: Favorites API (CRUD, per-user sets)
description: Add favorites CRUD endpoints to server/routes/user.js. Each user has a favorites array of named sets {id, name, castleIds}. Endpoints: GET/POST /api/user/favorites and PUT/DELETE /api/user/favorites/:id. Auth via Bearer token. No Angular changes.
close_reason: All four endpoints implemented and verified: GET/POST /favorites, PUT/DELETE /favorites/:id. Validation, auth, dedup, trimming all tested.

title: Phase 14.1: User API (register + me, token-based)
description: Implement a minimal user API on the Node server using JSON file storage (json-store). Two endpoints: POST /api/user/register (creates user, returns token) and GET /api/user/me (Bearer token auth, returns id + favorites). No database, no Angular changes.
close_reason: Implemented POST /api/user/register and GET /api/user/me. Bearer token auth, JSON file storage via json-store, all scenarios verified.

title: Phase 13.5: JSON store (atomic writes + mutex)
description: Implement safe JSON persistence utility for runtime data. Creates server/lib/json-store.js with readJson and writeJson functions. writeJson uses atomic writes (temp file + rename) and an in-process mutex (async-mutex) to prevent concurrent write corruption. No database, no extra abstractions — minimal utility only.
close_reason: Implemented server/lib/json-store.js with ESM syntax, atomic writes (temp file + rename), and in-process mutex via async-mutex. All verification tests pass.

title: Phase 13.1–13.2: Node server baseline (Express + static serving + SPA fallback)
description: Replace nginx runtime with a Node.js/Express server inside the Docker container (ADR-008). Serve Angular build output as static files, add SPA fallback for client-side routes, add /api/health endpoint. This is the foundational step for Phase 14 (user accounts) and Phase 15 (admin API).
close_reason: Server baseline implemented: server/index.js (Express, static, SPA fallback, /api/health), Dockerfile updated from nginx to node:18-alpine. Verified: /api/health 200, / 200, /castles/nl001 200.

title: Fix: Leaflet map not loading in production (CJS/ESM interop)
description: The map failed in production with TypeError: c.map is not a function. In the production bundle, await import('leaflet') returns the module namespace { default: L } rather than the Leaflet object directly. The dev build handles this transparently but the production esbuild output does not. Fixed in both castle-map.component.ts and castle-detail-page.component.ts by resolving the default export: const L = (leafletModule as any).default ?? leafletModule. This ensures L.map(), L.tileLayer(), L.circleMarker() etc. are always the real Leaflet functions regardless of build mode.

title: Fix: sequential image probing on castle detail page
description: The detail page rendered all 25 candidate image URLs as img tags simultaneously, causing up to 25 simultaneous HTTP requests. Most returned 404 for castles with fewer images (e.g. krak has 6 images but triggered requests for krak7 through krak25). Fixed by replacing the batch probe with a single sequential probe: only the next candidate URL is rendered; onImageLoad advances the probe index, onImageError stops probing. loadedImageUrls only contains successfully loaded images. The preload-img pattern is preserved but now a single element rather than a for loop.

title: 12.1 Add nginx.conf with SPA fallback and gzip
description: Without a custom nginx config, hard refresh on any client-side route (e.g. /castles/de-burg) returns a 404 because nginx looks for a real file at that path. This is a live production bug. Add nginx.conf to the repo with try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html so Angular routing works on direct URL access. Enable gzip compression for HTML, JS, CSS, and JSON. Update Dockerfile to COPY the config into /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf. This also supersedes the nginx gzip work planned in Phase 10.2.
close_reason: nginx.conf added with try_files SPA fallback and gzip; Dockerfile updated

Fix remaining dark mode issues on top100 surface

Keep scope to the top100 route template/styles and minimal related shared presentation surface only if needed for toggle/map contrast. Preserve architecture constraints and verify with the standard workflow.

Reuse existing test-setup patterns. No feature changes.
close_reason: Added user.service.spec.ts (17 specs) and favorites.service.spec.ts (12 specs). Suite passes 205/205.

title: Fix TD-1 Angular test-runner reliability
description: Restore reliable Angular unit-test execution by resolving the known TD-1 test-runner instability. The suspected root cause is castle-detail-page.component.spec.ts leaving an open HTTP request for /assets/data/castles_delta.json, causing HttpClientTestingBackend.verify() to cascade failures across the suite. npm test --watch=false --browsers=ChromeHeadless times out. Goal: make the suite produce a trustworthy regression signal.
close_reason: TD-1 already resolved by prior feature bead work. Suite runs 176/176 with npm test --watch=false --browsers=ChromeHeadless. No code changes needed.

title: Fix anonymous favorites bootstrap causing 401 + register 500
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: Opening Top1000 or Favorites as a first-time anonymous visitor can trigger GET /api/user/favorites returning 401 and POST /api/user/register returning 500. We need a minimal fix that keeps anonymous first-use flows working, makes registration first-run safe with runtime JSON storage under /data, prevents favorites loading from breaking page rendering, and retries favorites once after bootstrap without broad auth refactors.

description: Fix route param reactivity using toSignal (replace snapshot). Add removeCastleFromSet() to FavoritesService with optimistic update + rollback. Add optional (remove) output to grid and table components; wire up in favorites-detail.

title: Phase 14.7: Favorites detail view (/favorites/:id)
description: New route /favorites/:id shows castles in a favorites set. Click set on /favorites page navigates to detail. Reuses CastleGridComponent, CastleTableComponent, and ViewModeService. No new view components or API calls.

title: Phase 14.6 polish: token import validation + feedback
description: Refine token import: skip if same token, rollback on failed favorites load, show snackbar on success.

title: Phase 14.6: Token sharing (export + import via URL)
description: Allow users to export their favorites token as a shareable URL (?token=...) from the account page, and import it on another device via URL query param. No backend changes. Guards all browser APIs with isPlatformBrowser().

title: Phase 14.5 polish: optimistic rollback + favorites count + tooltip consistency
description: Polish the Phase 14.5 favorites UX: (1) rollback optimistic update on API failure in addCastleToSet, (2) show unique castle count in the show-favorites-only toggle label, (3) add matTooltip consistently to bookmark icons in grid and table.

title: 9.1 Dark mode with manual toggle and prefers-color-scheme
description: Add dark mode to the app. Use CSS custom properties for all palette colors. ThemeService reads localStorage and prefers-color-scheme to determine initial state, exposes a toggle(), and sets data-theme on <html>. Add a sun/moon icon button in the toolbar. Scope: custom tk-* palette only — Material component internals remain light-themed for now.

title: 9.2 Castle name autocomplete on castles filter page
description: Add MatAutocomplete dropdown to the Name field on the castles page. As the user types, show up to 8 matching castle names from the loaded dataset as suggestions. Selecting a suggestion sets the name filter to the exact castle name. This improves discoverability — users can find a specific castle without knowing the full name.

title: Virtual scroll table + Storybook stories for grid and table
description: Replace mat-table with CDK virtual scroll (CSS-grid rows + *cdkVirtualFor). Header sits above the viewport so it never scrolls away. BreakpointObserver drives responsive column hiding (no CSS display:none tricks). Write Storybook stories for castle-grid (Default + LargeList) and castle-table (Default + LargeList + SortBy). Update spec tests to match new DOM selectors.

title: Fix full-text search after lean/delta data split
description: The data split (topcastles-v83) moved description, remarkable, and website from castles.json to castles_delta.json. The top100 page search uses Object.values(c).some(v => String(v).includes(q)) over the lean signal, so those fields are silently excluded. Fix by adding a search_text field to the lean castles.json — a single concatenated string covering all searchable text (castle_name, country, place, region, area, founder, castle_type, castle_concept, description, remarkable). Update generate_lean_castles.js to build this field, update the search computed to use it.

title: Split data: lean castles.json at startup, lazy-load enriched on detail page
description: Currently castles_enriched.json (1.36 MB, ~282 kB gzipped) is loaded at app startup. Only the table/grid/filter/map fields are needed at startup. Four fields (description, remarkable, visitors, website) are detail-page-only. Ten enriched fields (Wikipedia/Wikidata) are also detail-page-only. Move to a two-tier load: lean castles.json (18 fields, ~100 kB gzipped) at startup; full castles_enriched.json loaded lazily on first detail page visit. Add a generate_lean_castles.js script that derives castles.json from castles_enriched.json. Update CastleService with loadEnrichedData() + getEnrichedCastle(). Update castle-detail-page to call loadEnrichedData().

title: 12.3 Load castles once at app startup via provideAppInitializer
description: Multiple components calling loadCastles() before the first HTTP response returns causes duplicate requests. The structural fix is to register CastleService.loadCastles() via provideAppInitializer() in app.config.ts so data is fetched once before any component renders. Audit and remove all component-level loadCastles() calls. Also delete NoCastleService and no-castle.service.ts — the no-castle dataset no longer exists and the service is dead code. Verify no imports remain before deleting.
close_reason: provideAppInitializer wired in app.config.ts; loadCastles returns Promise<void>; 6 component-level calls removed; NoCastleService left as-is (no_castles.json still exists and route is active)

title: 12.2 Prerender sharing routes for og: meta tag support
description: Link previews in WhatsApp, Slack, and social media require og:title, og:description, and og:image to be present in the raw HTML. Angular currently sets these via the Meta service (Phase 4.2) but they are only present after JS executes, so scrapers see a blank page. Write scripts/generate_prerender_routes.js that reads castles_enriched.json and outputs new_app/prerender-routes.txt listing /, /castles, all /countries/:slug (56 pages), and all /castles/:code (~1000 pages). Update angular.json prerender config to reference this file. Remove the serve:ssr:new_app npm script to clarify that server.ts is a build-time renderer only, not a deployed service.
close_reason: generate_prerender_routes.js writes 1006 routes; angular.json updated to routesFile; serve:ssr:new_app script removed

Expose the castle dataset for third-party developers, chatbots, and AI agents.

  - Documents schema, endpoints, example agent queries, and data freshness
- [x] **5.3** OpenAPI spec for the static API
  - `new_app/public/api/openapi.yaml` served at `/api/openapi.yaml`
  - Full Castle schema with all enrichment fields; all four endpoints described
- [x] **5.4** MCP (Model Context Protocol) server
  - `scripts/mcp-server.js` with `scripts/package.json` (`@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `zod`)
  - Tools: `search_castles`, `get_castle`, `list_countries`, `nearby_castles`

  - Configure in Claude Desktop via `claude_desktop_config.json`
---

- [ ] **8.2** Alternate-language Wikipedia fallback

- [ ] **8.3** Fill missing coordinates via Wikidata
  - 407 castles currently have no `latitude`/`longitude` — no location map or nearby-castles section
  - Update `enrich_wikidata.js` (or create `enrich_coordinates.js`) to query Wikidata `P625` (coordinate location) for castles missing coords
  - Fallback: use OpenStreetMap Nominatim geocoding on the castle name + country for remaining gaps
  - Coordinates are the prerequisite for map markers and the nearby-castles feature

  - `autoFit=true` so it fits all castles in that country
- [x] **7.2** Region detail page map
  - Add `<app-castle-map>` to the region detail page (currently has no map)
  - Filtered to castles in that region, auto-fit bounds
  - Note: regions share the country detail page via `?region=…`; using `cf.filtered()` scopes the map automatically
---

title: Phase 11 — Advanced & Optional
description:
Larger or more speculative improvements.
- [ ] **11.1** Castle comparison view
  - Select 2–3 castles and view them side-by-side in a comparison table
  - Pin comparison bar at bottom of the castles list page; accessible from any castle card/row
- [ ] **11.2** Structured data (JSON-LD)
  - Add `schema.org/LandmarksOrHistoricalBuildings` JSON-LD to each detail page
  - Enables Google rich results (knowledge panel, image carousel in search)
- [ ] **11.3** Accessibility audit
  - WCAG 2.1 AA audit: keyboard navigation, focus indicators, ARIA roles on map markers, colour contrast
  - Run `axe` or Lighthouse accessibility score ≥ 90 target
- [ ] **11.4** "Castle of the week" on homepage
  - Deterministic pick based on ISO week number + year (no server needed)
  - Prominent featured card on the home page with image and score
---

title: Phase 1 — Data Enrichment
description:
Foundation for Wikipedia/Wikidata display in Phase 2. Run scripts once, then re-run periodically to refresh data.
- [x] **1.1** Write Wikipedia enrichment script ([scripts/enrich_wikipedia.js](../scripts/enrich_wikipedia.js))
  - Iterates all castles from existing JSON
  - Calls `en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/page/summary/{castle_name}` with name fallbacks
  - Writes `wikipedia_extract`, `wikipedia_thumbnail`, `wikipedia_url` per castle
  - Output: `new_app/src/assets/data/castles_enriched.json`
- [x] **1.2** Write Wikidata enrichment script ([scripts/enrich_wikidata.js](../scripts/enrich_wikidata.js))
  - Resolves Wikidata QID via Wikipedia page title
  - Fetches: `P149` architectural style, `P1435` heritage designation, `P18` image, `P571` inception year
  - Appends `heritage_status`, `architectural_style`, `wikidata_id`, `inception_year`, `wikidata_image` per castle
  - Reads/writes `castles_enriched.json` in-place
- [x] **1.3** Update `Castle` model with optional enrichment fields ([new_app/src/app/models/castle.model.ts](../new_app/src/app/models/castle.model.ts))
  - Added: `wikipedia_extract?`, `wikipedia_thumbnail?`, `wikipedia_url?`, `wikidata_id?`, `architectural_style?`, `heritage_status?`, `inception_year?`, `wikidata_image?`
- [x] **1.4** Update `CastleService` to load enriched JSON ([new_app/src/app/services/castle.service.ts](../new_app/src/app/services/castle.service.ts))
  - Service now loads `castles_enriched.json`; all enrichment fields flow through automatically
- [x] **1.5** Run the enrichment scripts and commit `castles_enriched.json`
  - `node scripts/enrich_wikipedia.js`
  - `node scripts/enrich_wikidata.js`
  - Verify output, commit to repo
---

title: Dependencies
description:
```
1.1 → 1.2 → 1.5            (enrichment pipeline, sequential; 1.3 + 1.4 are code-only)
1.5 → 2.2, 2.3             (Wikipedia/Wikidata data must be committed before display)
2.1 (Leaflet install) → 3.2 (reuses same library)
1.5 → 5.1                  (enriched JSON must exist before slicing)
5.1 → 5.2, 5.3, 5.4        (endpoints must exist before describing them)
```
Independent (can be done in any order): 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1, 4.1, 4.2
```text
6.1 → 6.4        (clickable metadata needs filter query-param support already in place ✓)
8.1 → re-run 1.1 (overrides file feeds the Wikipedia script)
9.4 → submit to Google Search Console after deploy
10.1 before 10.3 (reduce bundle first, then add PWA to avoid caching bloated chunks)
11.2 pairs with 4.2 (both are SEO — do together)
```

title: Phase 6 — Detail Page Enhancements
description:
Items from `additional_improvements.md` not yet implemented.
- [x] **6.1** Clickable metadata on detail page
  - Region, castle type, building structure, condition and era become links/buttons
  - Clicking navigates to `/castles?country=…&castleType=…` (etc.) with the filter pre-applied
  - Era link goes to `/castles?era=12` — reuses existing query-param support
- [x] **6.2** In-place scrollable image strip
  - Replace the static thumbnail strip with a horizontally-scrollable carousel in the aside
  - Clicking a thumbnail makes it the featured image *in place* (no overlay) without leaving the page
  - Fullscreen lightbox remains available as an optional second action
- [x] **6.3** Extend nearby castles from 5 to 6
  - One-line change in `nearbyCastles` computed (`slice(0, 6)`)
  - Update spec assertions
- [x] **6.4** Thumbnail images in castle table and grid
  - Show `wikipedia_thumbnail` (or a local `/images/castles/small/{code}_small.jpg`) as a 60×60 cell in the table and a card header in the grid
  - Graceful fallback (castle icon) when no image is available
---

title: Phase 2 — Castle Detail Page
description:
- [x] **2.1** Leaflet map embed on detail page ([new_app/src/app/pages/castle-detail/](../new_app/src/app/pages/castle-detail/))

title: deploy.sh: add --autostart flag to control Docker restart policy
description: Add a CLI flag to deploy.sh so the Docker restart policy can be chosen at deploy time. Default keeps current behaviour (unless-stopped). The 'always' policy is needed when the container must survive a Docker daemon restart or a manual docker stop.

title: 11.5 Admin API for hot-swapping JSON data without rebuild
description: After running enrichment scripts locally, provide a way to push the updated castles_enriched.json to the live server without triggering a full Docker build and deploy. A minimal password-protected upload endpoint receives the new file, writes it to the shared data volume, then runs generate_lean_castles.js and generate_sitemap.js to re-derive castles.json, castles_delta.json, and sitemap.xml. The Angular app fetches these files at runtime, so visitors get fresh data on their next page load with no downtime. Prerendered HTML (og tags) stays stale until the next full build — acceptable for data-only updates. Implementation options: (1) small Node or Python sidecar container sharing the assets volume with nginx, (2) Synology Task Scheduler triggering the script on file-system change. Auth: Bearer token or basic auth checked by the sidecar.

title: 9.1 Dark mode
description: Add CSS custom properties for all colours in styles.scss. Auto-switch via prefers-color-scheme: dark media query; manual toggle in toolbar persisted to localStorage. Requires design decisions on the colour palette from 9.5 before implementation.

title: 9.2 Castle name autocomplete
description: Dropdown suggestion list while typing in the Name filter on the castles page. Use Angular Material MatAutocomplete — castle name data comes from CastleService.castles() signal. Requires UX pattern decisions from 9.5.

title: 9.5 Design refresh — Storybook + Figma exploration
description: Before implementing new visual features, establish a design baseline. Use Storybook (already in the stack) to review all existing components in isolation and identify gaps. Explore Figma or Penpot to design page layouts and component states. Candidate areas: typography scale, card layout, colour hierarchy, mobile responsiveness, empty states. Expand Storybook story coverage so every UI component has a story before the redesign begins. This issue gates the UI-heavy items 9.1, 9.2, 11.1, 11.4 which should not be built before design decisions are made.
close_reason: Refined roadmap UX design refresh priorities in docs/roadmap.md

title: 12.5 Scope getRegionSummaries() to a country parameter
description: getRegionSummaries() currently groups by region name globally. Two countries with a region of the same name (e.g. Normandy in France and a hypothetical Normandy elsewhere) would be merged into one summary. Regions are only meaningful within a country context — the UI never shows regions without a selected country. Change the signature to getRegionSummaries(country: string) and filter by country before grouping. This eliminates the collision without a composite key. Update all callers to pass the country argument.
close_reason: getRegionSummaries(country) now requires country; top-regions page gathers all regions via getCountries().flatMap; spec updated with scoping and collision tests

title: 12.4 Trust JSON sort order in CastleService, remove redundant sorts
description: castles_enriched.json is sorted by score_total descending. CastleService methods should rely on this rather than re-sorting on every call. Add a unit test asserting the JSON sort order to make the precondition explicit and catch accidental re-ordering from enrichment scripts. Simplify getAllByScore() to return this.castles() and getTopByScore(n) to return this.castles().slice(0, n). getTopByCountry() is already correct since filter() preserves the global sort order — add a comment. Keep the explicit sort in getTopByVisitors() as visitor score order differs from total score.
close_reason: getAllByScore and getTopByScore simplified; precondition comment added to service; spec updated with sort-order assertion; generate_prerender_routes.js warns if JSON is unsorted

title: 11.4 Castle of the week on homepage
description: Deterministic featured castle pick based on ISO week number + year (no server needed). Prominent featured card on the home page with image and score. New homepage UI element — requires design pass from 9.5 first.

title: 11.1 Castle comparison view
description: Allow users to select 2-3 castles and view them side-by-side in a comparison table. Pin a comparison bar at the bottom of the castles list page; accessible from any castle card or table row. Entirely new UI layout — requires design from 9.5 first.

title: Fix top countries spec row rendering failure

value: Synology NAS Docker data volume path: /volume1/docker/topcastles/data — used for persistent JSON storage (json-store.js). Mount as -v /volume1/docker/topcastles/data:/data in docker run.
